stage01_rawText.text:
Colin Kirby
kirbycolin26@gmail.com | github.com/KirbysGit | linkedin.com/in/colinwkirby/ | https://colinkirby.dev
Experience
Software Engineering Intern May 2024 – June 2025
BitGo | Python, Django, React, PostgreSQL, AWS EC2 Remote
• Built internal automation tools that allowed IT and compliance teams to schedule, monitor, and manage operational
workflows through a centralized web platform.
• Worked closely with design teams, engineers, and offshore developers to build and refine frontend workflows,
backend APIs, and real-time operational tooling.
• Developed task orchestration and backend processing workflows using Django, Celery, Redis, and PostgreSQL to
support scheduled jobs, logging, and live task updates.
• Developed centralized scheduling and monitoring tools that reduced manual task management, debugging, and log-
checking time by ~3 hours per week for internal IT teams.
Projects
Taylor.io – AI Resume Builder & Workflow Platform | Python, React, FastAPI, OpenAI API, Cursor
• Built an AI-powered resume platform that stores structured user career data and generates tailored resumes based on
specific job descriptions.
• Developed workflows that extract relevant keywords and requirements from job postings, then use OpenAI APIs to
generate targeted resume content from user profile data.
• Built FastAPI backend services for resume parsing, authentication, and real-time editing using PostgreSQL and React.
• Used AI-assisted coding workflows with tools like Cursor to rapidly prototype, iterate, and refine product features while
maintaining production-style architecture.
Centi – Personal Finance App | FastAPI, React, PostgreSQL, Plaid API | https://centi.dev
• Built a personal finance platform that aggregates bank accounts, transactions, and spending activity into one clear
dashboard, helping users understand their finances without manually sorting through multiple apps and statements..
• Developed transaction processing and categorization workflows using FastAPI, PostgreSQL, and Plaid APIs to organize
messy financial data into searchable spending insights and summaries.
• Created customizable React dashboards that let users explore spending trends, account activity, and financial patterns
in the way that makes the most sense to them.
SentimentTrader – Financial Sentiment & Market Analysis Pipeline | Python, SQL, FinBERT, XGBoost
• Built an end-to-end analytics pipeline processing 1K+ finance-related Reddit posts per run through automated ETL
workflows, combining market data and sentiment analysis to generate trading signals for users.
• Developed preprocessing and feature engineering workflows that transform unstructured discussions, engagement
metrics, and stock data into structured ticker-level indicators for analysis.
• Trained and evaluated XGBoost models achieving 55–70% ROC-AUC while comparing sentiment patterns against short-
term market movement.
ShelfVision – Dense Retail Shelf Object Detector | PyTorch, ResNet-50, FPN
• Built a retail analysis system trained on 11K+ images to detect and identify products in cluttered shelf environments.
• Developed preprocessing and training workflows that organize image data, generate training inputs, and improve
detection performance across different shelf layouts.
Education
University of Central Florida (GPA 3.78 / 4.0) August 2021 – May 2025
Bachelor of Science in Computer Engineering Orlando, FL
Honors & Awards: Dean’s List 2021, President’s Honor Roll 2023 – 2025, Pegasus Gold Scholarship
Clubs & Extracurriculars: AI @ UCF, Knight Hacks, SHPE @ UCF
Relevant Coursework: Massive Storage & Big Data, Enterprise Computing, Object-Oriented Software Design
Skills
Languages : Python, SQL, JavaScript, Java
Analysis & Data Tools : Pandas, NumPy, ETL Pipelines, XGBoost, FinBERT
Frameworks & Technologies : FastAPI, Django, React, Node.js, PostgreSQL, MySQL
AI Developer Tools : Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, AI-Assisted Development

experiences.title: Software Engineering Intern
experiences.description:
• Built internal automation tools that allowed IT and compliance teams to schedule, monitor, and manage operational workflows through a centralized web platform.
• Worked closely with design teams, engineers, and offshore developers to build and refine frontend workflows, backend APIs, and real-time operational tooling.

projects.title: Taylor.io – AI Resume Builder & Workflow Platform
description: Built an AI-powered resume platform that stores structured user career data and generates tailored resumes based on specific job descriptions.
Developed workflows that extract relevant keywords and requirements from job postings, then use OpenAI APIs to generate targeted resume content from user profile data.
Built FastAPI backend services for resume parsing, authentication, and real-time editing using PostgreSQL and React.
Used AI-assisted coding workflows with tools like Cursor to rapidly prototype, iterate, and refine product features while maintaining production-style architecture.

projects.title: Centi – Personal Finance App
description: Built a personal finance platform that aggregates bank accounts, transactions, and spending activity into one clear dashboard, helping users understand their finances without manually sorting through multiple apps and statements..
Developed transaction processing and categorization workflows using FastAPI, PostgreSQL, and Plaid APIs to organize messy financial data into searchable spending insights and summaries.
Created customizable React dashboards that let users explore spending trends, account activity, and financial patterns in the way that makes the most sense to them.

projects.title: SentimentTrader – Financial Sentiment & Market Analysis Pipeline
description: Built an end-to-end analytics pipeline processing 1K+ finance-related Reddit posts per run through automated ETL workflows, combining market data and sentiment analysis to generate trading signals for users.
Developed preprocessing and feature engineering workflows that transform unstructured discussions, engagement metrics, and stock data into structured ticker-level indicators for analysis.
Trained and evaluated XGBoost models achieving 55–70% ROC-AUC while comparing sentiment patterns against short-term market movement.

projects.title: ShelfVision – Dense Retail Shelf Object Detector
description: Built a retail analysis system trained on 11K+ images to detect and identify products in cluttered shelf environments.
Developed preprocessing and training workflows that organize image data, generate training inputs, and improve detection performance across different shelf layouts.
